  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Power-stealing thermostat configuration without a common (C) wire causing system cycling, chattering, or power starvation issues."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a dedicated C-wire from the HVAC control board to the thermostat.",
    "Alternatively, install a manufacturer-approved common wire adapter (add-a-wire kit) or a 24VAC isolation transformer."
  ],
  "rationale": "Power-stealing thermostats often cause erratic system behavior, short-cycling, or failure to power the thermostat screen when the system is running. A dedicated common (C) wire or an approved adapter must be installed before completing the thermostat installation to ensure stable 24VAC power.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals (Power and Wiring Requirements)",
    "NEC Article 725 (Class 2 Control Circuits)"
  ]
}
